generatedSentence: The old gaffer, a peculiar baker, lived shacked near the factory and shipyard, where mankind's vast mixture of tech often evoked a torpid melancholy. His small ketches, usually knotted securely, swayed in the indigo light. Luckily, his hacky amplifier, though often whacked, still worked, broadcasting distant jester static. He munched a thighbone-shaped pastry, his eyeglass glinting, a pocketknife at hand, ignoring the diffraction on his windows, as if milking peace from the heckled world. He dreamt of lost jewels. Multiple warnings flashed across the wardroom windows; a video jockey's scratchy voice announced a dreadful meltdown. A chill ran through the dorky gorillas usually found on the wayside. Their heartfelt cries could not dulcify the approaching uproar. An underwater power surge, like an eyewash of pure radiance, caused atrophy in nearby units, reducing their minutes of operation. Suddenly, a strange musket-like object appeared, threatening to steal even their last nickels, halting their jubilee. The inherent problem was the vintage robot, now offside, emanating an icky, jagged energy, its jumpsuit frayed. It had once roamed a wild jungle, but the oligarchs had given it specific tactics to bluff and hold the infinite gateway. The levitation device, resembling a vase, pulsed with electric power as it began a terrifying pickoff, its hose whipping. The dinosaur-like machine, a lanky mammal of destruction, was wakeful, ignoring all etiquette, fueled by pure pepperoni and a singular command. Through the crevice in the earth, a lone wildflower grew, an inchworms' solace, untouched by the previous rainfall. The tenant, clad in a khaki robe and kerchief, observed the scene with a tactical eyeglass, her mouth a firm line. She knew there was no unanimous agreement on fashion, but her talisman glowed with a new radiance. With a strategic swipe, she redirected the robot's energy. Mankind found wellness in the radiant burst, ecstatic that the breaker was finally fixed; the power had been restored.
panelSentences: The old gaffer, a peculiar baker, lived shacked near the factory and shipyard, where mankind's vast mixture of tech often evoked a torpid melancholy. He munched a thighbone-shaped pastry, his eyeglass glinting, a pocketknife at hand, ignoring the diffraction on his windows, as if milking peace from the heckled world. He dreamt of lost jewels.

Multiple warnings flashed across the wardroom windows; a video jockey's scratchy voice announced a dreadful meltdown. A chill ran through the dorky gorillas usually found on the wayside. Their heartfelt cries could not dulcify the approaching uproar. An underwater power surge, like an eyewash of pure radiance, caused atrophy in nearby units, reducing their minutes of operation. Suddenly, a strange musket-like object appeared, threatening to steal even their last nickels, halting their jubilee.

The inherent problem was the vintage robot, now offside, emanating an icky, jagged energy, its jumpsuit frayed. It had once roamed a wild jungle, but the oligarchs had given it specific tactics to bluff and hold the infinite gateway. The levitation device, resembling a vase, pulsed with electric power as it began a terrifying pickoff, its hose whipping. The dinosaur-like machine, a lanky mammal of destruction, was wakeful, ignoring all etiquette, fueled by pure pepperoni and a singular command.

Through the crevice in the earth, a lone wildflower grew, an inchworms' solace, untouched by the previous rainfall. The tenant, clad in a khaki robe and kerchief, observed the scene with a tactical eyeglass, her mouth a firm line. She knew there was no unanimous agreement on fashion, but her talisman glowed with a new radiance. With a strategic swipe, she redirected the robot's energy. Mankind found wellness in the radiant burst, ecstatic that the breaker was finally fixed; the power had been restored.

His small ketches, usually knotted securely, swayed in the indigo light. Luckily, his hacky amplifier, though often whacked, still worked, broadcasting distant jester static. He munched a thighbone-shaped pastry, his eyeglass glinting, a pocketknife at hand, ignoring the diffraction on his windows, as if milking peace from the heckled world. He dreamt of lost jewels.

Multiple warnings flashed across the wardroom windows; a video jockey's scratchy voice announced a dreadful meltdown. A chill ran through the dorky gorillas usually found on the wayside. Their heartfelt cries could not dulcify the approaching uproar. An underwater power surge, like an eyewash of pure radiance, caused atrophy in nearby units, reducing their minutes of operation. Suddenly, a strange musket-like object appeared, threatening to steal even their last nickels, halting their jubilee.

The inherent problem was the vintage robot, now offside, emanating an icky, jagged energy, its jumpsuit frayed. It had once roamed a wild jungle, but the oligarchs had given it specific tactics to bluff and hold the infinite gateway. The levitation device, resembling a vase, pulsed with electric power as it began a terrifying pickoff, its hose whipping. The dinosaur-like machine, a lanky mammal of destruction, was wakeful, ignoring all etiquette, fueled by pure pepperoni and a singular command.

Through the crevice in the earth, a lone wildflower grew, an inchworms' solace, untouched by the previous rainfall. The tenant, clad in a khaki robe and kerchief, observed the scene with a tactical eyeglass, her mouth a firm line. She knew there was no unanimous agreement on fashion, but her talisman glowed with a new radiance. With a strategic swipe, she redirected the robot's energy. Mankind found wellness in the radiant burst, ecstatic that the breaker was finally fixed; the power had been restored.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone female protagonist, with a determined expression, wearing a khaki robe and a glowing kerchief. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A gaffer, a baker, with a large thighbone-shaped pastry, looks through an eyeglass at indigo diffraction patterns on a window, inside a shacked, cluttered room near a factory, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Dorky gorillas on a wayside by large wardroom windows, reacting with heartfelt uproar to an underwater meltdown, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A lanky, vintage robot in a frayed jumpsuit, its levitation device resembling a vase, attacking a glowing infinite gateway, dynamic low angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The female protagonist in a khaki robe, with a glowing kerchief, executing a precise energy swipe against the robot, standing over a crevice with a wildflower, close-up.

definitions.definition: To live (in or with); to shack up.
To shed or fall, as corn or grain at harvest.
To feed in stubble, or upon waste.
To wander as a vagabond or tramp.
To hibernate; to go into winter quarters.
A precious or semi-precious stone; gem, gemstone.
A valuable object used for personal ornamentation, especially one made of precious metals and stones; a piece of jewellery.
Anything precious or valuable.
definitions.example: Galveston was the jewel of Texas prior to the hurricane.

definitions.definition: A bearing for a pivot in a watch, formed of a crystal or precious stone.
Any of various lycaenid butterflies of the genus Hypochrysops.
The clitoris.
To bejewel; to decorate or bedeck with jewels or gems.
Family jewels; testicles.
One who jests, jokes or teases.
A person in colourful garb and fool's cap who amused a medieval and early modern royal or noble court.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the Southeast Asian genus Symbrenthia.
A chief lighting technician for a motion-picture or television production.
A glassblower.
The bending of a wave around an obstacle.
The breaking up of an electromagnetic wave as it passes a geometric structure (e.g. a slit), followed by reconstruction of the wave by interference.
The bone that extends from the pelvis to the knee in humans; the femur.
To call out; to draw out or bring forth.
To cause the manifestation of something (emotion, picture, etc.) in someone's mind or imagination.
definitions.example: Being here evokes long forgotten memories.

definitions.definition: To elicit a response.
A person who bakes and sells bread, cakes and similar items.
A portable oven for baking.
A fore-and-aft rigged sailing vessel with two masts, main and mizzen, the mizzen being stepped forward of the rudder post.
A hangman.
To form into a knot; to tie with a knot or knots.
definitions.example: We knotted the ends of the rope to keep it from unravelling.

definitions.definition: To form wrinkles in the forehead, as a sign of concentration, concern, surprise, etc.
definitions.example: She knotted her brow in concentration while attempting to unravel the tangled strands.

definitions.definition: To unite closely; to knit together.
To entangle or perplex; to puzzle.
To form knots.
To knit knots for a fringe.
Full of knots; knotty.
Tied in knots.
Tangled, tangly, knotty, entangled, matted, snarled, unkempt, or uncombed.
Anything that amplifies, or makes something larger or more intense.
An adverb that adds intensity, such as "really" or "totally".
An appliance or circuit that increases the strength of a weak electrical signal without changing the other characteristics of the signal.
A portable encasement that houses a large speaker, used to amplify voices and musical instruments at live performances.
A lens that enlarges the field of vision.
Like a hack; amateurish.
Filthy or totally dirty.
Using, or characterised by, hacks: poorly designed workarounds.
Short and interrupted, broken, jerky; hacking.
definitions.example: A hacky cough. A hacky laugh. A hacky breath. A hacky howl.

definitions.definition: The human race in its entirety.
Men collectively, as opposed to all women.
Human feelings; humanity.
The act of mixing.
Something produced by mixing.
Something that consists of diverse elements.
A medicinal compound, typically a suspension of a solid in a solution
definitions.example: A teaspoonful of the mixture to be taken three times daily after meals

definitions.definition: A compound organ stop.
A cloth of variegated colouring.
A mix of different dry foods as a snack, especially chevda or Bombay mix.
A trading establishment, especially set up by merchants working in a foreign country.
The position or state of being a factor.
A building or other place where manufacturing takes place.
A device which produces or manufactures something.
A factory farm.
definitions.example: chicken factory; pig factory

definitions.definition: In a computer program or library, a function, method, etc. which creates an object.
A police station.
(of a configuration, part, etc.) Having come from the factory in the state it is currently in; original, stock.
definitions.example: See how there's another layer of metal there? That's not factory.

definitions.definition: A place where ships are built and repaired.
To hit, slap or strike.
To kill, bump off.
To share or parcel out; often with up.
definitions.example: to whack the spoils of a robbery

definitions.definition: To beat convincingly; to thrash.
(usually in the negative) To surpass; to better.
Tired; fatigued.
definitions.example: After that all-night party, we were all whacked.

definitions.definition: Intoxicated on drugs.
A purplish-blue colour
An indigo-colored dye obtained from certain plants (the indigo plant or woad), or a similar synthetic dye.
An indigo plant, such as from species in genera Indigofera, Amorpha (false indigo), Baptisia (wild indigo), and Psorothamnus and Dalea (indigo bush).
Having a deep purplish-blue colour
(Oxford University slang) An inferior racing boat, or one who rows in such a boat.
Unmoving
Dormant or hibernating
Lazy, lethargic or apathetic
An artificial lens, especially one of a pair.
A monocle.
An eyepiece.
The lens of the eye.

A knife with blades or tools that the user can fold or retract into its handle, and of a size small enough for carrying safely and handily in a pocket. Since the late 19th century the term "penknife" has not been distinct from "pocketknife", but the latter tends to refer to larger and more robust versions, sometimes with more attached tools, suited to heavier duty for casual or ad hoc applications outdoors or in workshops.
To question harshly in an attempt to find or reveal weaknesses.
To insult, tease, make fun of or badger.
definitions.example: Promise that you won't heckle me after my performance.

definitions.definition: To prepare flax for spinning using special combs called hackles
In a lucky manner; by good fortune; fortunately.
To express milk from (a mammal, especially a cow).
definitions.example: The farmer milked his cows.

definitions.definition: To draw (milk) from the breasts or udder.
definitions.example: to milk wholesome milk from healthy cows

definitions.definition: To express any liquid (from any creature).
To make excessive use of (a particular point in speech or writing, a source of funds, etc.); to exploit; to take advantage of (something).
definitions.example: When the audience began laughing, the comedian milked the joke for more laughs.

definitions.definition: (of an electrical storage battery) To give off small gas bubbles during the final part of the charging operation.
To single-mindedly masturbate a male to ejaculation, especially for the amusement and/or satisfaction of the masturbator/trix rather than the person masturbated.
definitions.example: Controlled milking can actually establish and consolidate a mistress’ dominance over her sub rather than diminish it.

definitions.definition: To chew with a grinding, crunching sound, and with the mouth closed — often used with on.
definitions.example: Jim was munching on a biscotti.

definitions.definition: To eat vigorously or with excitement.
definitions.example: Watching old Bill munch his pancakes makes me hungry!

definitions.definition: A whole number that can be divided by another number with no remainder.
definitions.example: 14, 21 and 70 are multiples of 7

definitions.definition: Price-earnings ratio.
One of a set of the same thing; a duplicate.
A single individual who has multiple personalities.
One of a set of siblings produced by a multiple birth.
A chain store.
A discovery resulting from the work of many people throughout history, not merely the work of the person who makes the final connection.
More than one (followed by plural).
definitions.example: My Swiss Army knife has multiple blades.

definitions.definition: Having more than one element, part, component, or function, having more than one instance, occurring more than once, usually contrary to expectations (can be followed by a singular).
definitions.example: It was a multiple pregnancy: the woman had triplets.

definitions.definition: A person who selects, or plays music videos either on television or at a disco.
A disruption, mark or shallow cut on a surface made by scratching.
definitions.example: Her skin was covered with tiny scratches.

definitions.definition: An act of scratching the skin to alleviate an itch or irritation.
definitions.example: The dog sat up and had a good scratch.

definitions.definition: A starting line (originally and simply, a line scratched in the ground), as in boxing.

A technical error of touching or surpassing the starting mark prior to the official start signal in the sporting events of long jump, discus, hammer throw, shot put, and similar. Originally the starting mark was a scratch on the ground but is now a board or precisely indicated mark.
The last riders to depart in a handicap race.
An aberration.
A horse withdrawn from a race prior to the start.
definitions.example: There were two scratches in race 8, which reduced the field from 9 horses to 7.

definitions.definition: Money.
A feed, usually a mixture of a few common grains, given to chickens.
(in the plural) Minute, but tender and troublesome, excoriations, covered with scabs, upon the heels of horses which have been used where it is very wet or muddy.
A scratch wig.
A genre of Virgin Islander music, better known as fungi.
To rub a surface with a sharp object, especially by a living creature to remove itching with nails, claws, etc.
definitions.example: Could you please scratch my back?

definitions.definition: To rub the skin with rough material causing a sensation of irritation; to cause itching.
definitions.example: I don't like that new scarf because it scratches my neck.

definitions.definition: To mark a surface with a sharp object, thereby leaving a scratch (noun).
definitions.example: A real diamond can easily scratch a pane of glass.

definitions.definition: To cross out, strike out, strike through some text on a page.
To produce a distinctive sound on a turntable by moving a vinyl record back and forth while manipulating the crossfader (see also scratching).
To commit a foul in pool, as where the cue ball is put into a pocket or jumps off the table.
definitions.example: Embarrassingly, he scratched on the break, popping the cue completely off the table.

definitions.definition: To score, not by skillful play but by some fortunate chance of the game.
To write or draw hastily or awkwardly; scrawl.
To dig or excavate with the claws.
definitions.example: Some animals scratch holes, in which they burrow.

definitions.definition: To dig or scrape (a person's skin) with claws or fingernails in self-defense or with the intention to injure.
definitions.example: The cat scratched the little girl.

definitions.definition: For or consisting of preliminary or tentative, incomplete, etc. work.
definitions.example: This is scratch paper, so go ahead and scribble whatever you want on it.

definitions.definition: Hastily assembled, arranged or constructed, from whatever materials are to hand, with little or no preparation
(from scratchpad) Relating to a data structure or recording medium attached to a machine for testing or temporary use.
(of a player) Of a standard high enough to play without a handicap, i.e. to compete without the benefit of a variation in scoring based on ability.
The side of a road or path.
definitions.example: Many wild flowers grew on the wayside.

definitions.definition: A rest area.
Situated near the side of a road or path.
definitions.example: We stopped at a wayside pub.

definitions.definition: The living quarters of a ship designated for the commissioned officers other than the captain.
The commissioned officers of a ship, excluding the captain.
definitions.example: The captain rarely referred to his wardroom for advice, and this resulted in their discontent.

definitions.definition: Having a temperature slightly higher than usual, but still pleasant; mildly hot.
definitions.example: The tea is still warm.

definitions.definition: Caring and friendly, of relations to another person.
definitions.example: We have a warm friendship.

definitions.definition: Having a color in the red-orange-yellow part of the visible electromagnetic spectrum.
Close, often used in the context of a game in which "warm" and "cold" are used to indicate nearness to the goal.
Fresh, of a scent; still able to be traced.
Communicating a sense of comfort, ease, or pleasantness
definitions.example: a warm piano sound

definitions.definition: Ardent, zealous.
definitions.example: a warm debate, with strong words exchanged

definitions.definition: Well off as to property, or in good circumstances; rich.
Requiring arduous effort.
The inedible parts of a grain-producing plant.
definitions.example: To separate out the chaff, early cultures tossed baskets of grain into the air and let the wind blow away the lighter chaff.

definitions.definition: Straw or hay cut up fine for the food of cattle.
Any excess or unwanted material, resource, or person; anything worthless.
Light jesting talk; banter; raillery.
Loose material, e.g. small strips of aluminum foil dropped from aircraft, intended to interfere with radar detection.
An opening, usually covered by one or more panes of clear glass, to allow light and air from outside to enter a building or vehicle.
An opening, usually covered by glass, in a shop which allows people to view the shop and its products from outside; a shop window.
The shutter, casement, sash with its fittings, or other framework, which closes a window opening.
A period of time when something is available.
definitions.example: launch window;  window of opportunity;  You have a two-hour window of clear weather to finish working on the lawn.

definitions.definition: A restricted range.

A rectangular area on a computer terminal or screen containing some kind of user interface, displaying the output of and allowing input for one of a number of simultaneously running computer processes.
A figure formed of lines crossing each other.
The time between first infection and detectability.
To furnish with windows.
To place at or in a window.
The largest of the apes, native to the forests of central Africa, and known for their trait of knuckle-walking.
A big and brutish man or a thug; a goon or ruffian.
A powerful person or organization; a heavyweight or behemoth.
Like a dork.
To sweeten the taste of.
To make sweeter or more pleasant.
To neutralise the acidity of.
To mollify or make peaceful.
definitions.example: He knew all the things to say to dulcify his mother.

definitions.definition: A soothing medicated lotion for the eyes
Nonsense; flattery; pretentiousness.
To fool with nonsense or flattery.
(intelligence) To keep information secret by sending false information to many people in one's own organization, and correct information to a select few.
Ability to do or undergo something.
(social) Ability to coerce, influence or control.
(physical) Effectiveness.
A large amount or number.
Any of the elementary forms or parts of machines: three primary (the lever, inclined plane, and pulley) and three secondary (the wheel-and-axle, wedge, and screw).
definitions.example: the mechanical powers

definitions.definition: A measure of the effectiveness that a force producing a physical effect has over time. If linear, the quotient of: (force multiplied by the displacement of or in an object) ÷ time. If rotational, the quotient of: (force multiplied by the angle of displacement) ÷ time.

A product of equal factors (and generalizations of this notion): x^n, read as "x to the power of n" or the like, is called a power and denotes the product x \times x \times \cdots \times x, where x appears n times in the product; x is called the base and n the exponent.
Cardinality.
The probability that a statistical test will reject the null hypothesis when the alternative hypothesis is true.
(in plural) In Christian angelology, an intermediate level of angels, ranked above archangels, but exact position varies by classification scheme.
To provide power for (a mechanical or electronic device).
definitions.example: This CD player is powered by batteries.

definitions.definition: To hit or kick something forcefully.
To enable or provide the impetus for.
Impressive.
(baby-talk) Urine.
(baby-talk) The genitalia (as used for urination), especially the penis.
The quality of being radiant, shining, bright or splendid.
The flux of radiation emitted per unit solid angle in a given direction by a unit area of a source.
A reduction in the functionality of an organ caused by disease, injury or lack of use.
To wither or waste away.
To cause to waste away or become abortive; to starve or weaken.
Felt or believed deeply and sincerely.
definitions.example: She expressed her heartfelt sympathies at the death of his mother.

definitions.definition: Severe overheating of the core of a nuclear reactor resulting in the core melting and radiation escaping.
definitions.example: Four years have passed since the meltdown at the Chernobyl nuclear plant, but the grim legacy of the Soviet catastrophe is still unfolding. https//web.archive.org/web/20041130094534/http://www.time.com/time/daily/chernobyl/chernobyl.index.html

definitions.definition: A situation being likened to a nuclear meltdown; a crisis.
definitions.example: Computer engineers were at a loss last night to explain why the Government had been hit by arguably the worst electronic meltdown in the history of Whitehall. https//web.archive.org/web/20041209011615/http://news.independent.co.uk/uk/politics/story.jsp?story=587262

definitions.definition: A tantrum.

A kind of firearm formerly carried by the infantry of an army, originally fired by means of a match, or matchlock, for which several mechanical appliances (including the flintlock, and finally the percussion lock) were successively substituted; ultimately superseded by the rifle.
definitions.example: Sam, Sam, pick up thy musket.

definitions.definition: A male Eurasian sparrowhawk.
The opening of a creature through which food is ingested.
definitions.example: "Open your mouth and say 'aah'," directed the doctor.

definitions.definition: The end of a river out of which water flows into a sea or other large body of water.
definitions.example: The mouth of the river is a good place to go birdwatching in spring and autumn.

definitions.definition: An outlet, aperture or orifice.
definitions.example: The mouth of a cave

definitions.definition: A loud or overly talkative person.
definitions.example: My kid sister is a real mouth; she never shuts up.

definitions.definition: (saddlery) The crosspiece of a bridle bit, which enters the mouth of an animal.
A principal speaker; one who utters the common opinion; a mouthpiece.
Cry; voice.
Speech; language; testimony.
A wry face; a grimace; a mow.
To speak; to utter.
definitions.example: He mouthed his opinions on the subject at the meeting.

definitions.definition: To make the actions of speech, without producing sound.
definitions.example: The prompter mouthed the words to the actor, who had forgotten them.

definitions.definition: To utter with a voice that is overly loud or swelling.
To pick up or handle with the lips or mouth, but not chew or swallow.
definitions.example: The fish mouthed the lure, but didn't bite.

definitions.definition: To take into the mouth; to seize or grind with the mouth or teeth; to chew; to devour.
To form or cleanse with the mouth; to lick, as a bear licks her cub.
To make mouths at.
A unit of time equal to sixty seconds (one-sixtieth of an hour).
definitions.example: You have twenty minutes to complete the test.

definitions.definition: A short but unspecified time period.
definitions.example: Wait a minute, I’m not ready yet!

definitions.definition: A unit of angle equal to one-sixtieth of a degree.
definitions.example: We need to be sure these maps are accurate to within one minute of arc.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural, minutes) A (usually formal) written record of a meeting or a part of a meeting.
definitions.example: Let’s look at the minutes of last week’s meeting.

definitions.definition: A unit of purchase on a telephone or other network, especially a cell phone network, roughly equivalent in gross form to sixty seconds' use of the network.
definitions.example: If you buy this phone, you’ll get 100 free minutes.

definitions.definition: A point in time; a moment.
A nautical or a geographic mile.
An old coin, a half farthing.
A very small part of anything, or anything very small; a jot; a whit.
A fixed part of a module.
A while or a long unspecified period of time
definitions.example: Oh, I ain't heard that song in a minute!

definitions.definition: Of an event, to write in a memo or the minutes of a meeting.
definitions.example: I’ll minute this evening’s meeting.

definitions.definition: To set down a short sketch or note of; to jot down; to make a minute or a brief summary of.
The official notes kept during a meeting.
A moderate, but uncomfortable and penetrating coldness.
definitions.example: There was a chill in the air.

definitions.definition: A sudden penetrating sense of cold, especially one that causes a brief trembling nerve response through the body; the trembling response itself; often associated with illness: fevers and chills, or susceptibility to illness.
definitions.example: Close the window or you'll catch a chill.   I felt a chill when the wind picked up.

definitions.definition: An uncomfortable and numbing sense of fear, dread, anxiety, or alarm, often one that is sudden and usually accompanied by a trembling nerve response resembling the body's response to biting cold.
definitions.example: Despite the heat, he felt a chill as he entered the crime scene.   The actor's eerie portrayal sent chills through the audience.   His menacing presence cast a chill over everyone.

definitions.definition: An iron mould or portion of a mould, serving to cool rapidly, and so to harden, the surface of molten iron brought in contact with it.
The hardened part of a casting, such as the tread of a carriage wheel.
A lack of warmth and cordiality; unfriendliness.
Calmness; equanimity.
A sense of style; trendiness; savoir faire.
To lower the temperature of something; to cool
definitions.example: Chill before serving.

definitions.definition: To become cold
definitions.example: In the wind he chilled quickly.

definitions.definition: To harden a metal surface by sudden cooling
To become hard by rapid cooling
To relax, lie back
definitions.example: Chill, man, we've got a whole week to do it; no sense in getting worked up.

definitions.definition: To "hang", hang out; to spend time with another person or group. Also chill out.
definitions.example: Hey, we should chill this weekend.

definitions.definition: To smoke marijuana
definitions.example: On Friday night do you wanna chill?

definitions.definition: To discourage, depress
definitions.example: Censorship chills public discourse.

definitions.definition: Moderately cold or chilly.
definitions.example: A chill wind was blowing down the street.

definitions.definition: Unwelcoming; not cordial.
definitions.example: Arriving late at the wedding, we were met with a chill reception.

definitions.definition: Calm, relaxed, easygoing.
definitions.example: Paint-your-own ceramics studios are a chill way to express yourself while learning more about your date's right brain.

definitions.definition: "Cool"; meeting a certain hip standard or garnering the approval of a certain peer group.
definitions.example: That new movie was chill, man.

definitions.definition: Okay, not a problem.
definitions.example: "Sorry about that." "It's chill."

definitions.definition: Tumultuous, noisy excitement.
Loud confused noise, especially when coming from several sources.
A loud protest, controversy, outrage
To throw into uproar or confusion.
To make an uproar.
Underlying water or body of water, for example in an aquifer or the deep ocean
A type of lure which lies beneath the water surface.
To water or irrigate insufficiently
definitions.example: Care must be taken not to underwater houseplants in the summer.

definitions.definition: Beneath the surface of the water, or of or pertaining to the region beneath the water surface
Beneath the water line of a vessel
Under water.
Having negative equity; owing more on an asset than its market value
definitions.example: We've been underwater on our mortgage ever since the housing crash.

definitions.definition: Going beneath the surface of the water
A silvery elemental metal with an atomic number of 28 and symbol Ni.
A coin worth 5 cents.
(by extension) Five dollars.
(by extension) Five hundred dollars.
(sometimes the nickel or the hot nickel) Interstate 5, a highway that runs along the west coast of the United States.
A playing card with the rank of five
A five-year prison sentence.
A defensive formation with five defensive backs, one of whom is a nickelback, instead of the more common four.
To plate with nickel.
(Jewish history) A special year of emancipation supposed to be kept every fifty years, when farming was abandoned and Hebrew slaves were set free.
A 25th, 40th, 50th, 60th or 70th anniversary.
A special year (originally held every hundred years, then fifty, and then fewer) in which remission from sin could be granted as well as indulgences upon making a pilgrimage to Rome.
A time of celebration or rejoicing.
An occasion of mass manumission from slavery.
A period of fifty years; a half-century.
Awake; not sleeping.
Sleepless.
Vigilant and alert; watchful.
Naturally as part or consequence of something.
Unpleasantly sticky; yucky; disgusting.
definitions.example: I stepped in something icky and it smells terrible.

definitions.definition: Excessively sentimental.
Unwell or upset; in a bad state of mind or health.
definitions.example: I'm not going to work today. I just feel icky.

definitions.definition: A playful or competitive activity.
A video game.
(nearly always singular) A field of gainful activity, as an industry or profession.
definitions.example: He's in the securities game somehow.

definitions.definition: Something that resembles a game with rules, despite not being designed.
definitions.example: In the game of life, you may find yourself playing the waiting game far too often.

definitions.definition: An exercise simulating warfare, whether computerized or involving human participants.
Wild animals hunted for food.
definitions.example: The forest has plenty of game.

definitions.definition: (used mostly of males) The ability to seduce someone, usually by strategy.
definitions.example: He didn't get anywhere with her because he had no game.

definitions.definition: Mastery; the ability to excel at something.
A questionable or unethical practice in pursuit of a goal; a scheme.
definitions.example: You want to borrow my credit card for a week? What's your game?

definitions.definition: To gamble.
To play card games, board games, or video games.
To exploit loopholes in a system or bureaucracy in a way which defeats or nullifies the spirit of the rules in effect, usually to obtain a result which otherwise would be unobtainable.
definitions.example: We'll bury them in paperwork, and game the system.

definitions.definition: (of males) To perform premeditated seduction strategy.
An offside play.
The side of a road vehicle furthest from the kerb: the right side if one drives on the left of the road.
definitions.example: My offside wing mirror got snapped off.

definitions.definition: The right-hand side of a working animal such as a horse or bullock, especially when in harness.
(of a canal) The side opposite the towpath.
In an illegal position ahead of the ball, puck, etc.
To the side of the road, past the curb and sidewalk, e.g. an offside diner (restaurant.)
Unfavourably located, from the point of view of the player taking a finesse.
To the side of a boat, opposite the primary side on which one paddles.
Something that breaks.
A machine for breaking rocks, or for breaking coal at the mines
The building in which such a machine is placed.
A person who specializes in breaking things.
(chiefly in the plural) A wave breaking into foam against the shore, or against a sandbank, or a rock or reef near the surface, considered a useful warning to ships of an underwater hazard
A breakdancer.
A user of CB radio.
One who works in shipbreaking.
(chiefly in the plural) A problem or difficulty in a particular field of study.
Posing a problem; having or suffering from problem(s):
Only affirming the possibility that a predicate be actualised.
A play in which a pitcher throws a live ball to a fielder so that the fielder can tag out a baserunner who has moved away from the base
A long loose outer garment, often signifying honorary stature.
The skin of an animal, especially the bison, dressed with the fur on, and used as a wrap.
A wardrobe, especially one built into a bedroom.
The largest and strongest tobacco leaves.
To clothe; to dress.
To put on official vestments.
The amount of rain that falls on a single occasion
The occurrence of liquid precipitation, the fall of rain.
Tall, slim, and rather ungraceful or awkward.
An animal of the class Mammalia, characterized by being warm-blooded, having hair and feeding milk to its young.
A vertebrate with three bones in the inner ear and one in the jaw.
Based on unanimity, assent or agreement.
definitions.example: The debate went on for hours, but in the end the decision was unanimous.

definitions.definition: Sharing the same views or opinions, and being in harmony or accord.
definitions.example: We were unanimous: the President had to go.

definitions.definition: A particular, minute unit of mass, defined differently for different substances, but so that varying substances of the same general type have the property that one international unit of the one has the same effect on the human body as one international unit of the other.
Oneness, singularity, seen as a component of a whole number; a magnitude of one.
A standard measure of a quantity.
definitions.example: The centimetre is a unit of length.

definitions.definition: The number one.
An organized group comprising people and/or equipment.
definitions.example: He was a member of a special police unit.

definitions.definition: A member of a military organization.
definitions.example: The fifth tank brigade moved in with 20 units. (i.e., 20 tanks)

definitions.definition: Any military element whose structure is prescribed by competent authority, such as a table of organization and equipment; specifically, part of an organization.
An organization title of a subdivision of a group in a task force.
A standard or basic quantity into which an item of supply is divided, issued or detailed. In this meaning, also called unit of issue.

With regard to Reserve Components of the Armed Forces, denotes a Selected Reserve unit organized, equipped, and trained for mobilization to serve on active duty as a unit or to augment or be augmented by another unit. Headquarters and support functions without wartime missions are not considered units.
The identity element, neutral element.
An element having an inverse, an invertible element; an associate of the unity.
In an adjunction, a natural transformation from the identity functor of the domain of the left adjoint functor to the composition of the right adjoint functor with the left adjoint functor.

A volume of rock or ice of identifiable origin and age range that is defined by the distinctive and dominant, easily mapped and recognizable petrographic, lithologic or paleontologic features (facies) that characterize it.
An item which may be sold singly.
definitions.example: We shipped nearly twice as many units this month as last month.

definitions.definition: A unit of alcohol.
One kilowatt-hour (as recorded on an electricity meter).

A measure of housing equivalent to the living quarters of one household; an apartment where a group of apartments is contained in one or more multi-storied buildings or a group of dwellings is in one or more single storey buildings, usually arranged around a driveway.
A gold coin of the reign of James I, worth twenty shillings.
A work unit.
A physically large person.
For each unit.
definitions.example: We have to keep our unit costs down if we want to make a profit.

definitions.definition: Having a size or magnitude of one.
The yield of grapes or wine from a vineyard or district during one season.
Wine, especially high-quality, identified as to year and vineyard or district of origin.
The harvesting of a grape crop and the initial pressing of juice for winemaking.
The year or place in which something is produced.
To harvest (grapes).
To make (wine) from grapes.
Of or relating to a vintage, or to wine identified by a specific vintage.
Having an enduring appeal; high-quality.
Classic (such as watches, video or computer games from the 1980s and early 1990s, old magazines, etc.).
To cut unevenly.
To tease.
Unevenly cut; having the texture of something so cut.
Having a rough quality.
Of an array, having a different cardinality in each dimension, such that a representation on paper would appear uneven.
Endlessness, unlimitedness, absence of a beginning, end or limits to size.
A number that has an infinite numerical value that cannot be counted.
An idealised point which is said to be approached by sequences of values whose magnitudes increase without bound.

A number which is very large compared to some characteristic number. For example, in optics, an object which is much further away than the focal length of a lens is said to be "at infinity", as the distance of the image from the lens varies very little as the distance increases further.
The symbol ∞.
An entrance capable of being blocked by use of a gate.
Any point that represents the beginning of a transition from one place or phase to another.
A point at which freight moving from one territory to another is interchanged between transportation lines.

(digital communications) In wireless internet, an access point with additional software capabilities such as providing NAT and DHCP, which may also provide VPN support, roaming, firewalls, various levels of security, etc.
(digital communications) To make available via a gateway, or access point.
Inclined to drowse; heavy with sleepiness
definitions.example: I was feeling drowsy and so decided to make a cup of coffee to try to wake myself up.

definitions.definition: Causing someone to fall sleep or feel sleepy; lulling; soporific.
definitions.example: It was a warm, drowsy summer afternoon.

definitions.definition: Boring.
Dull; stupid.
A maneuver, or action calculated to achieve some end.
A maneuver used against an enemy.
A sequence of moves that limits the opponent's options and results in an immediate and tangible advantage, typically in the form of material.
The military science that deals with achieving the objectives set by strategy.
The employment and ordered arrangement of forces in relation to each other.
A magical object providing protection against ill will, or the supernatural, or conferring the wearer with a boon such as good luck, good health, or power(s).

The forms required by a good upbringing, or prescribed by authority, to be observed in social or official life; observance of the proprieties of rank and occasion; conventional decorum; ceremonial code of polite society.
The customary behavior of members of a profession, business, law, or sports team towards each other.
A label used to indicate that a letter is to be sent by airmail.
A machine built to carry out some complex task or group of tasks by physically moving, especially one which can be programmed.
An intelligent mechanical being designed to look like a human or other creature, and usually made from metal.
A person who does not seem to have any emotions.
A traffic light (from earlier robot policeman).
A theodolite which follows the movements of a prism and can be used by a one-man crew.
A style of dance popular in disco in which the dancer imitates the stiff movements of a stereotypical android robot.
A flexible tube conveying water or other fluid.
A stocking-like garment worn on the legs; pantyhose, women's tights.
Close-fitting trousers or breeches, reaching to the knee.
To water or spray with a hose.
To deliver using a hose.
To provide with hose (garment)
To attack and kill somebody, usually using a firearm.
To trick or deceive.
To break a computer so everything needs to be reinstalled; to wipe all files.
To cause an unfair disadvantage to a player or team through poor officiating; especially, to cause a player or team to lose the game with an incorrect call.
The raising of something, such as a body, without apparent physical cause, allegedly using the power of the mind
The suspension of something via technical means without any mechanical support, such as by magnetism
An upright open container used mainly for displaying fresh, dried, or artificial flowers.
The body of the Corinthian capital.
A one-piece item of clothing originally used by parachutists
A similar item of clothing used for outdoor sports such as skiing
In scientific usage, any of the animals belonging to the clade Dinosauria, especially those that existed during the Triassic, Jurassic and Cretaceous periods and are now extinct.
In non-scientific usage, any non-avian dinosaur.
Any extinct reptile, not necessarily belonging to Dinosauria, that existed between about 230 million and 65 million years ago.
A person or organisation that is very old, has very old-fashioned views, or is not willing to change and adapt.
Anything no longer in common use or practice.
A spicy salami-style Italian-American sausage.
Pizza with only tomato sauce, cheese and pepperoni toppings.
definitions.example: Yo, Tony, gimme a slice of pepperoni and a coke.

definitions.definition: To wander or travel freely and with no specific destination.
To use a network or service from different locations or devices.
To transmit (resources) between different locations or devices, to allow comparable usage from any of them.
To range or wander over.
definitions.example: Gangs of thugs roamed the streets.

definitions.definition: A dull, yellowish-brown colour, the colour of dust.
Khaki green, a dull green colour.
A strong cloth of wool or cotton, often used for military or other uniforms.
A soldier wearing a khaki uniform.
A British person (from the colour of the uniform of British troops, originally in the Second Boer War; compare rooinek). (In this sense the plural generally is khakies.)
Khaki clothing or uniform, commonly in the plural.
definitions.example: he was dressed in khaki, he was wearing his khakies

definitions.definition: Dust-coloured; of the colour of dust.
The set of letters used when writing in a language.
definitions.example: In the first year of school, pupils are taught to recite the alphabet.

definitions.definition: A writing system in which letters represent phonemes. (Contrast e.g. logography, a writing system in which each character represents a word, and syllabary, in which each character represents a syllable.)
A typically finite set of distinguishable symbols.
definitions.example: Let L be a regular language over the alphabet \Sigma.

definitions.definition: An individual letter of an alphabet; an alphabetic character.
The simplest rudiments; elements.
To designate by the letters of the alphabet; to arrange alphabetically.
A grasp or grip.
definitions.example: Keep a firm hold on the handlebars.

definitions.definition: An act or instance of holding.
definitions.example: Can I have a hold of the baby?

definitions.definition: A place where animals are held for safety
An order that something is to be reserved or delayed, limiting or preventing how it can be dealt with.
definitions.example: Senator X placed a hold on the bill, then went to the library and placed a hold on a book.

definitions.definition: Something reserved or kept.
definitions.example: We have a hold here for you.

definitions.definition: Power over someone or something.
The ability to persist.
The property of maintaining the shape of styled hair.
A position or grip used to control the opponent.
definitions.example: He got him in a tight hold and pinned him to the mat.

definitions.definition: (exercise) An exercise involving holding a position for a set time
The percentage the house wins on a gamble, the house or bookmaker's hold.
definitions.example: The House Hold on the game is 10,000, this is the amount of decision or risk the house wishes to assume.

definitions.definition: The wager amount, the total hold.
definitions.example: As of Monday night the total Melbourne Cup hold was $848,015

definitions.definition: An instance of holding one's service game, as opposed to being broken.
The part of an object one is intended to grasp, or anything one can use for grasping with hands or feet.
A fruit machine feature allowing one or more of the reels to remain fixed while the others spin.
A pause facility.
The queueing system on telephones and similar communication systems which maintains a connection when all lines are busy.
A statistic awarded to a relief pitcher who is not still pitching at the end of the game and who records at least one out and maintains a lead for his team.
A region of airspace reserved for aircraft being kept in a holding pattern.
To grasp or grip.
definitions.example: Hold the pencil like this.

definitions.definition: To contain or store.
definitions.example: This package holds six bottles.

definitions.definition: (heading) To maintain or keep to a position or state.
(heading) To maintain or keep to particular opinions, promises, actions.
To win one's own service game.
To take place, to occur.
To organise an event or meeting (usually in passive voice).
definitions.example: Elections will be held on the first Sunday of next month.

definitions.definition: To derive right or title.
In a food or drink order at an informal restaurant etc., requesting that a component normally included in that order be omitted.
definitions.example: A martini, please, and hold the olive.

definitions.definition: To be in possession of illicit drugs for sale.
A quick grab, bat, or other motion with the hand or paw; a sweep.
A strong blow given with a sweeping motion, as with a bat or club.
An act of interacting with a touch screen by drawing the finger rapidly across it.
An act of passing a swipecard through a card reader.
A rough guess; an estimate or swag.
definitions.example: Take a swipe at the answer, even if you're not sure.

definitions.definition: An attack, insult or critical remark.
definitions.example: The politician took a swipe at his opponents.

definitions.definition: Poor, weak beer or other inferior alcoholic beverage; rotgut.
To grab or bat quickly.
definitions.example: The cat swiped at the shoelace.

definitions.definition: To strike with a strong blow in a sweeping motion.
To scan or register by sliding (a swipecard etc.) through a reader.
definitions.example: He swiped his card at the door.

definitions.definition: To interact with a touch screen by drawing one's finger rapidly across it.
definitions.example: Swipe left to hide the toolbar.

definitions.definition: To draw (one's finger) rapidly across a touch screen.
To steal or snatch.
definitions.example: Hey! Who swiped my lunch?

definitions.definition: A large, undeveloped, humid forest, especially in a tropical region, that is home to many wild plants and animals; a tropical rainforest.
Any uncultivated tract of forest or scrub habitat.
A place where people behave ruthlessly, unconstrained by law or morality.
definitions.example: It’s a jungle out there.

definitions.definition: An area where hobos camp together.
A migrant camp.
A style of electronic music related to drum and bass.
(Israel) A desert region.
Dense rough.
A hairy vulva.
(Of musical beat, rhythm, etc.) resembling the fast-paced drumming of traditional peoples of the jungle.
The quality or state of being in good health.
The process of learning about and engaging in behaviors that are likely to result in optimal health.
A shocker: a report of a crime written in a provokingly lurid style.
A journal or broadsheet printing such reports.
A shocking or sensational crime.
Full of something causing dread, whether
Full of dread, whether
Dreadfully.
A member of an oligarchy; someone who is part of a small group that runs a country.
(especially Russia, USA, Europe, or China) A very rich person, particularly with political power; a plutocrat.
(cosmogony) A protoplanet formed during oligarchic accretion.
(usually with definite article) Electricity; the electricity supply.
definitions.example: We had to sit in the dark because the electric was cut off.

definitions.definition: An electric car.
An electric toothbrush.
A substance or object which can be electrified; an insulator or non-conductor, like amber or glass.
Fencing with the use of a body wire, box, and related equipment to detect when a weapon has touched an opponent.
Of, relating to, produced by, operated with, or utilising electricity; electrical.
Of or relating to an electronic version of a musical instrument that has an acoustic equivalent.
Being emotionally thrilling; electrifying.
(familiar) mother.
An adult female owner of a pet.
To care in a motherly way.
Comfort or consolation in a time of loneliness or distress.
definitions.example: You cannot put a monetary value on emotional solace.

definitions.definition: A source of comfort or consolation.
To give solace to; comfort; cheer; console.
To allay or assuage.
To take comfort; to be cheered.
A piece of cloth used to cover the head; a bandana.
To cover with a kerchief.
A narrow crack or fissure, as in a rock or wall.
To crack; to flaw.
A current (constantly changing) trend, favored for frivolous rather than practical, logical, or intellectual reasons.
Popular trends.
definitions.example: Check out the latest in fashion.

definitions.definition: A style or manner in which something is done.
The make or form of anything; the style, shape, appearance, or mode of structure; pattern, model; workmanship; execution.
definitions.example: the fashion of the ark, of a coat, of a house, of an altar, etc.

definitions.definition: Polite, fashionable, or genteel life; social position; good breeding.
definitions.example: men of fashion

definitions.definition: To make, build or construct, especially in a crude or improvised way.
To make in a standard manner; to work.
To fit, adapt, or accommodate to.
To forge or counterfeit.
A negating expression; an answer that shows disagreement or disapproval
A vote not in favor, or opposing a proposition
definitions.example: The workers voted on whether to strike, and there were thirty "yeses" and two "nos".

definitions.definition: (now only used with different, with comparatives more and less, and informally with certain other adjectives such as good and fun) Not, not at all.
definitions.example: I just want to find out whether she's coming or no. (Scotland)

definitions.definition: Without
Like
A wild (uncultivated) flowering plant.
A flower from such a plant.
The larva of a moth of the family Geometridae.
To move in a looping fashion, like an inchworm.
To crawl or creep slowly.
One who pays a fee (rent) in return for the use of land, buildings, or other property owned by others.
One who has possession of any place.
One who holds a property by any kind of right, including ownership.
Any of a number of customers serviced through the same instance of an application.
To hold as, or be, a tenant.
To inhabit.
(in the plural) Transports of delight; words or actions performed in a state of ecstasy.
A person in a state of ecstasy.
Feeling or characterized by ecstasy.
Extremely happy.
Relating to, or caused by, ecstasy or excessive emotion.
definitions.example: ecstatic gaze; ecstatic trance

definitions.definition: An act of bluffing; a false expression of the strength of one's position in order to intimidate; braggadocio.
definitions.example: That is only bluff, or a bluff.

definitions.definition: An attempt to represent oneself as holding a stronger hand than one actually does.
definitions.example: John's bet was a bluff: he bet without even so much as a pair.

definitions.definition: The card game poker.
One who bluffs; a bluffer.
An excuse.
To make a bluff; to give the impression that one's hand is stronger than it is.
definitions.example: John bluffed by betting without even a pair.

definitions.definition: (by analogy) To frighten or deter with a false show of strength or confidence; to give a false impression of strength or temerity in order to intimidate and gain some advantage.
definitions.example: The government claims it will call an election if this bill does not pass. Is it truly ready to do so, or is it bluffing?

definitions.definition: To take advantage by bluffing.
definitions.example: We bluffed our way past the guards.
